goal: Fill every empty pipe gap with a pipe that matches both the color system and the visible shape of the missing segment.

pipe_types.description: A pipe segment with two opposite openings.
pipe_types.matching_gap: A missing segment where the surrounding pipe path continues straight through the gap.
pipe_types.color_rule: Use the straight pipe from the same color system as the surrounding path.

pipe_types.description: A pipe segment with two adjacent openings.
pipe_types.matching_gap: A missing segment where the surrounding pipe path turns ninety degrees.
pipe_types.color_rule: Use the corner pipe from the same color system as the surrounding path.

pipe_types.description: A pipe segment with three openings.
pipe_types.matching_gap: A missing segment where three pipe paths meet.
pipe_types.color_rule: Use the three-way pipe from the same color system as the surrounding paths.

pipe_types.description: A pipe segment with four openings.
pipe_types.matching_gap: A missing segment where paths continue in four directions.
pipe_types.color_rule: Use the cross pipe from the same color system as the surrounding paths.

pipe_types.description: A straight pipe segment with a valve attached.
pipe_types.matching_gap: It behaves like a straight segment, but this specific puzzle does not require it.
pipe_types.color_rule: It still belongs to a color system, but it is not part of the current solution set.
pipe_types.current_puzzle_usage: Available as an unused extra piece.

rules: A correct placement matches the pipe color to the surrounding color system.
A correct placement matches the pipe type to the shape of the missing pipe segment.
Pipe colors do not mix across systems.
Pipe type is determined by the number and arrangement of openings.
Not every available pipe must be used.
Disabled pipe types are not required for the current puzzle.
visible_clues: Color is visible on the pipe and on the surrounding pipe system.
A straight gap looks like a path continuing in one line.
A corner gap looks like a path that bends.
A three-way gap looks like three paths meeting.
A cross gap looks like four paths meeting.
The player can infer the correct pipe from nearby pipe color, nearby pipe direction, and the number of paths entering the gap.
common_mistakes: Using the correct shape from the wrong color system.
Using the correct color with the wrong shape.
Trying to use every available pipe even when some are extras.
Repeating the same placement without comparing the pipe shape to the surrounding paths.
Choosing based only on color and ignoring how many directions the gap connects.
Treating the StraightWithValve pipe as required even though it is not needed in this puzzle.
success_conditions: Every placed pipe matches the color of its surrounding system.
Every placed pipe matches the shape of its gap.
The scene state reports zero incorrect slots.

examples.correct_match: A same-color gap where the path continues straight.
examples.incorrect_match: A bend, junction, intersection, or gap in the other color system.
examples.reason: A straight pipe has two opposite openings.

examples.correct_match: A same-color gap where the pipe path turns.
examples.incorrect_match: A straight segment, T junction, intersection, or gap in the other color system.
examples.reason: A corner pipe has two adjacent openings.

examples.correct_match: A same-color gap where three paths meet.
examples.incorrect_match: A straight segment, bend, four-way intersection, or gap in the other color system.
examples.reason: A three-way pipe has three openings.

examples.correct_match: A same-color gap where four paths meet.
examples.incorrect_match: A straight segment, bend, T junction, or gap in the other color system.
examples.reason: A cross pipe has four openings.
